data.title: TheStreet Pro Analysts Say, "It's a Stock Picker's Market – Here Are 4 to Consider"
data.description:
This market is anything but easy. One day, we're setting a new high and the next day, we might be down 2%. That's volatility for ya. Not only volatility, but there's also lots of sector rotation happening. The stocks that are up this year are, generally, not the ones that have been hot over the last few years.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Magnificent 7 aren't looking so magnificent in 2026. NVIDIA (NVDA) is basically flat, Microsoft (MSFT) is off 18%, Tesla (TSLA) is down 9%. I could go on…

What's doing well? As we've discussed, Consumer Defensive stocks are rocketing. Like Walmart (WMT), Pepsi (PEP), and Clorox (CL), up 16%, 13%, and 21%, respectively. Industrials are mostly green, too. Caterpillar (CAT) is up more than 30%.

There's always a bull market somewhere. It's just not always obvious, and the Mag 7 stocks, with their huge weight in the indices, has lulled us into thinking that investing is easy.

Now, however, it's a stock picker's market. And that's where TheStreet Pro comes in. As we've been discussing, our team of traders and analysts offers amazing trade ideas. Combine that with the ability to do additional research on TipRanks, and you've got the start of a winning portfolio! The best part is that today is a great time to become a member of TheStreet Pro. An annual subscription is currently half off during our President's Day sale. You can learn more here.

Today, I'd like to share a few ideas from our team. Some trades take time to work, and we'll start with two of those. McDonald's is a new name for us to discuss. Cullinan Therapeutics, however, is one that I'd like to follow up on. Afterwards, we'll review two bounce candidates with chart patterns that make for a good potential opportunity.

McDonalds Hamburgers Heat Up While Wendy's Gets Fried?

One of the things I like about Ed Ponsi's work is that he combines charts with financial data. He's always looking for an edge, and that benefits his readers. On Tuesday, Ed published "McDonald's Sees Price Target Changes Amid Wendy's Issues" over on TheStreet Pro.

First of all, did you know that McDonald's (MCD) has returned more than 850% in the last 20 years? And that it's largely gone sideways for the last four? Well, shares are already up 8% in 2026 and Ed thinks that's just the beginning.
data.author: Jason Meshnick, The Street Pro

data.title: D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) Executive Sells Shares after RSU Vesting: Here's What It Means for Investors
data.description:
D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) disclosed a small insider stock sale in a recent SEC Form 4 filing. The transaction came from Diane Nguyen, the company's EVP and Chief Legal Officer. The sale took place on February 13, 2026, and involved 1,451 shares at an average price of about $19.76. Following the sale, Nguyen retained 538,138 shares in the company.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Importantly, this was not a typical insider sale based on personal market views. Instead, the filing explains that the shares were sold only to meet tax rules tied to vesting stock awards. The document states that the sale was required "to cover the statutory tax withholding obligations" linked to restricted stock units. It also notes that this "does not represent a discretionary trade" by the executive.

Meanwhile, QBTS shares dropped a significant 6.25% on Tuesday, closing at $18.44. Yesterday, Infleqtion, now known as ColdQuanta, Inc. (INFQ), started trading on the NYSE, causing all pure-play stocks to drop, including IonQ (IONQ), Rigetti (RGTI), QCI (QUBT), and Arqit Quantum (ARQQ).
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Palantir Stock's "Winning Streak Will Not Endure," Says Big Short's Michael Burry, He Forecasts $46 PLTR Price
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is currently the target of a massive bet by Michael Burry, the famous investor from "The Big Short." Burry has published a 10,000-word report on his Substack claiming that the company's recent success is not built to last. He believes the stock price could crash by as much as 66%, which would bring it down to about $46 per share. This warning comes at a tough time for the company, as its stock has already fallen by more than 26% over the last few weeks. While many people are excited about AI, Burry says the current "winning streak will not endure" and argues the company's valuation has drifted far away from its actual business results.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Burry Challenges Corporate Culture and Accounting

The core of Burry's attack focuses on the idea that the stock trades on "aura" rather than financial reality. He has raised serious questions about how Palantir reports its profits and manages its costs. He suggests that the company's cost allocation, specifically how it counts the labor used to set up its software, makes its profit margins look more like a high-end software company than they actually are. In his critique, Burry also questions the company's leadership style, asking: "You think it is helpful having a fluorescent green praying mantis coming into their offices telling them about German philosophy?" He argues that investors are paying for a version of the firm that simply does not exist.

Analysts Defend Palantir's Rapid Growth

Despite the long and detailed report, many Wall Street analysts are sticking by the company. Analysts at D.A. Davidson, led by Gil Luria, read the entire piece and said they found no new reason to worry about the stock. They argue that Burry's view is flawed because it ignores how fast the company is actually growing in the real world. Palantir expects 2026 revenue to hit $7.19 billion, which is a 60% increase from last year. Its U.S. commercial business is also booming, with revenue recently jumping by 137%. These supporters believe the company's $7 billion in cash and low debt provide plenty of protection even if the market gets rocky.

Technical Patterns Suggest More Downside

Investors who follow chart patterns are noticing a head-and-shoulders reversal on the PLTR chart, a pattern that Burry himself pointed out on social media. This technical signal often happens right before a big drop in price. Burry noted that the first major floor for the stock is around $80, but it could eventually land between $45 and $60. With the stock currently trading around $131.41, a move to Burry's target would be a painful hit for anyone who bought in during the recent AI rally.

Is Palantir Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, PLTR stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 11 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell rating assigned in the last three months. At $191.25, the average 12-month PLTR price target implies a 43.8% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Morgan Stanley Says These 2 Momentum Stocks Could Climb Even Higher
data.description:
Investors are often drawn to stocks that are already outperforming. Even though the familiar caveat reminds us that historical returns don't ensure future gains, sustained rallies tend to attract capital.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That dynamic underpins momentum investing. The strategy can be effective when price strength reflects solid fundamentals – companies operating in attractive markets, generating healthy profits, and executing against durable business models. In such cases, upward trends are not just technical patterns but signals of underlying business momentum.

Conviction tends to build further when leading Wall Street firms share that positive view. A review of the TipRanks database highlights two momentum stocks that have earned support from Morgan Stanley. The bank's analysts see compelling reasons for further upside, making these names worth a closer examination.

TeraWulf (WULF)

The first stock we'll look at, WULF, represents a company that lives on the leading edge of today's tech world – a combination of data centers, high performance computing, and bitcoin mining. TereWulf has more than 30 years of experience drawing value from energy, and has five utility-scale data center facilities in operation. Two of these were built on the sites of coal-fired power plants, one on a repurposed industrial site; those locations bring advantages, allowing the company to make use of preexisting energy infrastructure without causing environmental damage.

TeraWulf's five data campus locations have an aggregate total of nearly 2.8 gigawatts of infrastructure capacity. The company's Lake Mariner facility, the 750 megawatt campus in Barker, New York, is also the site of TeraWulf's bitcoin mining operations. Approximately 90% of the power drawn by this facility comes from zero-carbon sources.

Outside of bitcoin mining, TeraWulf makes its data centers available for contract operations, giving customers access to high-end, AI-capable computing power based on low-cost and sustainable energy production, and supported by redundant fiber access and networking, and built in full compliance with energy, physical, and regulatory security standards.

The AI boom of recent years has put a premium on quality data centers, and TeraWulf has been a direct beneficiary of that. The company's stock has gained an impressive 243% over the past 12 months, the uptick coming against a backdrop of rising revenues.

Covering this stock for Morgan Stanley, analyst Stephen Byrd notes that the company provides a sound model for investors to gain access to the high-demand data center sector.

"For investors seeking maximum exposure to DC growth, and who share our thesis that there is a large excess of demand for compute relative to supply, WULF is an attractive way to achieve such exposure," Byrd opined.

"TeraWulf has a strong track record of signing agreements with data center customers, and the management team has extensive experience in building a wide range of power infrastructure assets…. We project that the company will achieve success in converting the sites that currently do not have a Bitcoin-to-DC contract, at a present value that equates to ~$8/ watt (or an un-discounted value of $10/watt). We also assume that management achieves a 50% success rate in achieving the lower end of its targeted annual DC project growth of 250 MW/year in 2028-32," the analyst added.

Byrd follows up these comments by rating WULF as Overweight (i.e., Buy) with a $37 price target that implies a robust 129% gain in the coming months. (To watch Byrd's track record, click here)

Overall, the Street gives this stock a unanimous Strong Buy consensus rating, based on 6 recent positive reviews. The stock is priced at $16.18 and its $24.20 average price target indicates room for a 49.5% gain by this time next year. (See WULF stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/18/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Climb Ahead of FOMC Minutes
data.description:
U.S. stock futures climbed early Wednesday as traders anticipated the minutes from the Federal Reserve's January meeting. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.69%, 0.61%, and 0.51%, respectively, at 5:46 a.m. EST on February 18.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed higher.  The S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq Composite all edged up roughly 0.1% on the day. The software sector, battered by AI disruption fears, dropped further yesterday. CrowdStrike (CRWD) tumbled 3.6%, while ServiceNow (NOW) shed 1.1%.

Turning to economic data, traders are focused on the FOMC minutes due later today. Also, data on housing starts, building permits, and durable goods orders will be published today. Of more importance is Friday's release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index for December, the Fed's favorite gauge, which will reveal more about the economy's health.

On the earnings front, Booking Holdings (BKNG), Occidental Petroleum (OXY), Analog Devices (ADI), and Carvana (CVNA) will release their results today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.07%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $62.61 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,913 per ounce on Wednesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today amid positive global market developments. Moreover, the UK's inflation rate fell to 3% in January as per official stats.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded higher on February 18, with Mainland Chinese and Hong Kong markets remaining closed for the Lunar New Year.

Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei gained 1.02%, and the Topix increased 1.21%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth
